enhancements.title: Guided demo now hides the 'LIVE' dock during Chat mode runs
enhancements.detail: Chat mode demos still grade the visit behind the scenes just like before, but they no longer show the glowing 'LIVE' listening dock and headset icon — that visual is now reserved for real Live Listen sessions. The end-of-visit recap for a chat-mode demo also uses a neutral heading instead of 'Live Listen ended', so it doesn't look like you were on a live call when you weren't.
enhancements.answer: When you run a demo in Chat mode, Rep Assist still grades the transcript against the playbook the same way it always has, but it no longer shows the 'LIVE' dock or glowing headset — that indicator now only appears for actual Live Listen sessions. The wrap-up recap for a chat-mode demo also gets a neutral header instead of 'Live Listen ended', so the demo reads cleanly without implying you were live on a call.

walkthrough.intro: Try the guided demo and see the difference between running it in Chat mode versus Live Listen mode now that the visuals match what's really happening.

steps.title: Open the menu drawer
steps.detail: Tap the ☰ button and look for 'Run a demo' under Quick start.

steps.title: Pick Chat mode or Live Listen mode
steps.detail: Choose Chat mode for a quiet, transcript-only demo, or Live Listen mode to see the full live transcript and coaching flow.

steps.title: Notice the LIVE dock is gone in Chat mode
steps.detail: In Chat mode, grading still happens in the background, but you won't see the glowing 'LIVE' strip or headset icon — that only shows up in real Live Listen sessions now.
steps.tip: If you're demoing for a new hire, point out that the LIVE indicator is specific to Live Listen mode.

steps.title: Let the visit play out at a readable pace
steps.detail: Rep Assist walks through check-in, the conversation, and checkout or a resolution ticket at a slower, easier-to-follow speed.

steps.title: Review the wrap-up recap
steps.detail: A chat-mode demo shows a neutral summary heading instead of 'Live Listen ended', since no live session was actually visible.

steps.title: Check the Playbook grade
steps.detail: The demo visit still gets scored like a real one, so you can see what a strong or weak visit looks like.

enhancements.title: Checkout now works like a real store POS — with bill review, signatures, and phone handoff
enhancements.detail: The in-chat cart is now a full point-of-sale flow. Adding a device automatically recommends device protection (you can remove it), and you can add perks, accessories, and trade-in credit, then choose pickup or ship. When you're ready to check out, a 'View Together' card shows the current bill vs. the new bill vs. blended monthly cost, plus everything due today — taxes, activation, and accessories. The cart tray now pops open briefly whenever you make a change, then automatically tucks itself back down after a couple seconds so it doesn't block the chat — you can still tap to keep it open manually. You can finish on your screen with a simulated payment and captured signature, or text/QR-code the checkout straight to the customer's own phone so they can review and sign it themselves while you both watch it update live.
enhancements.answer: Once you've built a cart, tap to check out and Rep Assist shows a 'View Together' card comparing the customer's current bill, the new bill, and the blended monthly cost, along with everything due today like taxes, activation fees, and accessories. The cart tray briefly flashes open every time you add or change something, then automatically collapses back down after about 2-3 seconds so it stops covering the chat — you can tap it to keep it open if you want it visible. From there you can finish the sale on your own screen with a simulated payment and captured signature, or hand it off by texting a link or showing a QR code so the customer finishes it on their own phone.

walkthrough.intro: Learn how to build a full cart with add-ons, work with the auto-collapsing tray, and finish checkout either yourself or by handing it to the customer's phone.

steps.title: Build the cart as usual
steps.detail: Say things like 'add a line' or 'upgrade this phone' and Rep Assist builds the cart in the drawer as before.

steps.title: Notice the cart tray flash open on changes
steps.detail: Every time you add or edit something, the tray pops fully open so you can see the update, then tucks itself back down to a compact bar after a couple seconds.
steps.tip: Tap the tray if you want to keep it open manually — that overrides the auto-collapse.

steps.title: Review the extras that come with a device
steps.detail: When a device is added, device protection is now recommended by default — remove it if the customer doesn't want it — and you can also add perks, accessories, and trade-in credit.

steps.title: Choose pickup or ship
steps.detail: Pick how the customer will get their device or accessories.

steps.title: Open the View Together bill review
steps.detail: Tap to check out and Rep Assist shows current vs. new vs. blended monthly cost, plus everything due today including taxes and activation.
steps.tip: Walk the customer through this card out loud — it's designed to be reviewed together.

steps.title: Finish it yourself, or hand it to their phone
steps.detail: Either continue on your screen with a simulated payment and signature, or tap to send a text link or show a QR code so the customer finishes checkout on their own phone.
steps.tip: Both screens update live and stay in lock-step, so you can watch their progress if they take over.

enhancements.title: See the whole store queue at a glance — and now assist with one tap
enhancements.detail: A badge in the top bar always shows how many customers are waiting, being helped, and picking up online orders, updating on its own. Tap it to open the full queue with waiting customers, in-progress visits, pickup orders, and today's appointments — and you can now tap Assist right on a waiting customer's row to claim them without leaving the queue view.
enhancements.answer: The top bar badge shows live counts like waiting, active, and ISPU (in-store pickup), and tapping it opens a drawer with waiting customers, in-progress visits, pickup orders, and today's remaining appointments. You can now tap Assist directly on a waiting customer's row inside that drawer to claim them and jump straight into chat with their details already filled in — no need to go find them somewhere else first.

walkthrough.intro: Learn how to check the queue badge and claim a waiting customer directly from it.

steps.title: Glance at the top bar badge
steps.detail: You'll see a compact count like '3 wait / 2 active / 1 ISPU' that refreshes automatically.

steps.title: Tap the badge
steps.detail: This opens the Live Queue drawer with the full breakdown.

steps.title: Find a waiting customer
steps.detail: Look in the Waiting section for who's next.

steps.title: Tap Assist on their row
steps.detail: This claims the customer and drops you straight into chat with their name and details already filled in.
steps.tip: You no longer need to leave the queue drawer to start helping someone.

steps.title: Check pickup orders and appointments too
steps.detail: Scroll down to see online orders needing pickup and today's remaining scheduled appointments.

enhancements.title: Menu moved into one global drawer, plus a quick 'new chat' button
enhancements.detail: Every screen now opens the same slide-out menu — tap the ☰ in the top corner or the one next to your message box while chatting. Inside you'll find Workspace, Analytics, and Admin sections, plus a Quick start area with the same shortcuts you used to see as chat buttons. There's also a + button next to your message box to jump straight into a fresh conversation.
enhancements.answer: Rep Assist now uses one navigation drawer everywhere in the app — open it from the ☰ in the top bar on any screen, or the ☰ next to your message box while chatting. Inside, options are grouped into Workspace, Analytics, and Admin, with a Quick start section holding shortcuts like Front Desk, Look Up, and Run a demo. We also added a + button beside the composer to instantly start a new conversation.

walkthrough.intro: Learn where the app's menu options live and how to start a fresh conversation quickly.

steps.title: Tap the ☰ in the top bar
steps.detail: No matter what screen you're on, tap the ☰ icon to slide out the global menu.

steps.title: Or tap the ☰ next to your message box
steps.detail: While in a chat, you can also open the same drawer using the ☰ button beside the composer.

steps.title: Browse the sections
steps.detail: Inside you'll see Workspace, Analytics, and Admin groups, plus a Quick start section with shortcuts.

steps.title: Tap + to start a new conversation
steps.detail: Next to your message box, tap + to jump straight into a brand-new chat.

enhancements.title: Live Listen now scores your visits and coaches you afterward
enhancements.detail: After you end a Live Listen session, Rep Assist grades the conversation against the store's official playbook and gives you a 1-5 star score with what you did well and what to work on. A Coaching tile lets you pull up past graded visits and get a suggested script for doing better next time.
enhancements.answer: When you stop a Live Listen session, Rep Assist compares the conversation to the current playbook guidelines and shows a Playbook Score card with a star rating, a quick headline, and a breakdown of strengths and gaps. Tap the Coaching tile in the menu to revisit any recent graded visit and get a personalized tip plus a suggested script — especially helpful if you missed positioning something the customer was eligible for.

walkthrough.intro: Here's how to see your Playbook Score after a visit and pull up coaching to improve next time.

steps.title: Start a Live Listen session
steps.detail: Tap Listen in the chat composer and pick the checked-in customer you're helping.

steps.title: Have your normal conversation
steps.detail: Talk with the customer as usual — Live Listen quietly follows along.

steps.title: End the session
steps.detail: Tap Stop when you're done talking with the customer.

steps.title: Check your Playbook Score card
steps.detail: A card appears showing your star rating, a quick headline, and strengths vs. gaps.
steps.tip: Read the gaps section closely — it often points out an opportunity you didn't mention.

steps.title: Open the Coaching tile
steps.detail: Tap 🎯 Coaching in the menu drawer to see your recent graded visits.

steps.title: Pick a visit for a suggested script
steps.detail: Select any past visit to get a specific tip and a suggested script for handling it better next time.

enhancements.title: Spot sales opportunities before you even start talking
enhancements.detail: Rep Assist flags customers who may be eligible for things like an upgrade promo, Fiber Home Internet, or Fixed Wireless Internet with a 💡 badge in the check-in queue, and Live Listen calls out those opportunities right when you start the session.
enhancements.answer: Look for the 💡 badge on a customer's entry in the front-desk queue card — it means Rep Assist has spotted something they may qualify for. When you start Live Listen with that customer, it will remind you out loud what to look for, and if you don't end up mentioning it, your visit score and coaching feedback will call that out afterward.

walkthrough.intro: Learn to spot the opportunity badge and make sure you position it during the visit.

steps.title: Open the queue card
steps.detail: Pull up the front-desk queue to see who's checked in and waiting.

steps.title: Look for the 💡 badge
steps.detail: A lightbulb badge next to a customer's name means they may be eligible for an upgrade promo or a home internet offer.

steps.title: Claim the customer
steps.detail: Tap Assist on their entry, or start a Live Listen session with them.

steps.title: Listen for the callout
steps.detail: When Live Listen starts, it reminds you which opportunity to bring up.
steps.tip: Mention it early — it's easy to forget once the conversation moves on.

steps.title: Check your coaching feedback afterward
steps.detail: If you didn't position the opportunity, your Playbook Score will flag it as a gap.

enhancements.title: Live Listen gets a docked transcript, smarter lookups, and visit-summary emails
enhancements.detail: The live transcript stays pinned above your message box instead of scrolling away. Live Listen also uses the customer's known account or order info automatically, and when you end a session you can send the customer a short AI-written visit summary by email with one tap.
enhancements.answer: When you check in a customer with an account or order number, Live Listen carries that straight into the conversation so it doesn't need to ask again. The transcript stays docked above the chat box so it won't scroll out of view. When you stop a session, Rep Assist writes a quick customer-friendly recap and shows it as a card — tap 'Send visit summary' to email that recap to the customer.

walkthrough.intro: See how the improved Live Listen experience keeps the transcript visible and can email the customer a recap.

steps.title: Check in the customer with their info
steps.detail: Add their account or order number when checking them in, if you have it.

steps.title: Start Live Listen
steps.detail: Tap Listen and select the customer — their info carries in automatically.

steps.title: Watch the docked transcript
steps.detail: It stays pinned above your message box the whole time.

steps.title: Stop the session
steps.detail: Tap Stop once the conversation wraps up.

steps.title: Review the visit summary card
steps.detail: Rep Assist shows a short, customer-friendly recap of what happened.

steps.title: Send it to the customer
steps.detail: Tap 'Send visit summary' to email the recap directly.
steps.tip: Make sure the customer's email preference is turned on in Settings.

enhancements.title: Check in walk-in customers and manage the front-desk queue
enhancements.detail: A Check In option lets you log a walk-in customer's name, phone, and reason for the visit, plus their account or order number if you have it. Everyone waiting shows up in a queue card, and tapping Assist on an entry pulls their info straight into chat.
enhancements.answer: Use the Check In button to log a walk-in customer with their visit reason, contact info, and account or order number if known, and they'll appear in the store's queue card in arrival order. Tap Assist on their entry to claim it — Rep Assist drops you into chat with their name and details already filled in.

walkthrough.intro: Learn how to check in a walk-in customer and pick them up from the queue.

steps.title: Open the menu drawer
steps.detail: Tap ☰ and choose Front Desk to see the Check In option.

steps.title: Tap Check In
steps.detail: This opens the intake form for a walk-in customer.

steps.title: Fill in the customer's details
steps.detail: Enter their name, phone number, reason for the visit, and account/order number if known.

steps.title: Submit the check-in
steps.detail: The customer appears in the store's queue card in arrival order.

steps.title: Find them in the queue
steps.detail: Open the queue card or the Live Queue badge to see everyone waiting.

steps.title: Tap Assist to claim them
steps.detail: This drops you straight into chat with their details already filled in.
steps.tip: No need to re-ask for info they already gave at check-in.

suggestions: How does the new checkout bill review work?
What does the 💡 opportunity badge mean in the queue?
How do I see my Playbook Score after a Live Listen session?
